Question: What kind of wife did Yahweh tell Hosea to take for himself?
Response: Yahweh told him to take for himself a wife who was a prostitute.

Question: Why should Hosea call the name of his first son Jezreel?
Response: Hosea should call his name Jezreel. For in a little while Yahweh will punish the house of Jehu for the bloodshed at Jezreel.

Question: On whom will Yahweh have mercy?
Response: Yahweh will have mercy on the house of Judah.

Question: Why should Hosea call the name of his second son Lo Ammi?
Response: Hosea should call his name Lo Ammi, for the Israelites were no longer his people.

Question: What will be said to those who formerly were not Yahweh’s people?
Response: It will be said to them that they were people of the living God.

Question: What will Yahweh do if Israel does not put away her prostitution?
Response: If Israel does not put away her prostitution, Yahweh will strip her naked and show her nakedness as on the day that she was born.

Question: Why will Yahweh not have any mercy on Israel’s children?
Response: Yahweh will not have any mercy on her children, for they were children of prostitution.

Question: Why will Israel return to her first husband?
Response: Israel will return to her first husband, for was it better for her then than it now.

Question: Who lavished on Israel silver and gold?
Response: Yahweh lavished on her silver and gold.

Question: What will Yahweh do to Israel in the sight of her lovers?
Response: Yahweh will strip her naked in the sight of her lovers.

Question: What wages did Israel’s lovers give her?
Response: Israel’s lovers gave her vines and fig trees.

Question: How will the prostitute answer Yahweh after he goes to win her back?
Response: She will answer him as she did in the days of her youth, as in the days that she came out of the land of Egypt.

Question: In that day, what will Israel call Yahweh?
Response: She will call him “My husband,” and no longer, “My Baal.”

Question: What will Yahweh make his people do?
Response: He will make them lie down in safety.

Question: For how long will Yahweh promise to be his people’s husband?
Response: Yahweh will promise to be their husband forever.

Question: What will the new wine and the oil answer?
Response: The new wine and the oil will answer Jezreel.

Question: What will Yahweh say to those who were not his people?
Response: Yahweh will say to those who were not his people, “You are my people.”

Question: In what way must Hosea love his wife?
Response: Hosea must love her just as Yahweh loves the people of Israel.

Question: For how much did Yahweh buy Israel?
Response: He bought her for fifteen pieces of silver and a homer and a lethek of barley.

Question: After the people of Israel live for many days without a king, who will they seek?
Response: After the people of Israel live for many days without a king, they will seek Yahweh their God and David their king.

Question: Against whom did Yahweh have a lawsuit?
Response: Yahweh had a lawsuit against the inhabitants of the land.

Question: What was happening to every one who was living in the land?
Response: Every one who was living in it was wasting away.

Question: Who will stumble by night?
Response: The prophets will stumble by night.

Question: Why will Yahweh forget the children of the people of Israel?
Response: Yahweh will forget the children of the people of Israel because they forgot the Law of God.

Question: How will the people act?
Response: They will act like the priests.

Question: What things took away the understanding of the people?
Response: Sexual promiscuity, wine, and new wine took away their understanding.

Question: Why will Yahweh not punish the people’s daughters when they choose to commit sexual immorality?
Response: Yahweh will not punish the people’s daughters when they choose to commit sexual immorality, for the men also give themselves to the prostitutes.

Question: Where should the people not go?
Response: They should not go to Gilgal or Beth Aven.

Question: What did Ephraim do even when their strong drink was gone?
Response: Ephraim continued to commit adultery even when their strong drink was gone.

Question: Where did the rebels stand?
Response: The rebels stood deep in slaughter.

Question: What will not allow Ephraim to turn to their God?
Response: Ephraim’s deeds will not allow them to turn to their God.

Question: Why will Israel, Ephraim, and Judah not find Yahweh?
Response: Israel, Ephraim, and Judah will not find Yahweh, for he has withdrawn himself from them.

Question: Who declared that Ephraim will become a desolation?
Response: Yahweh declared that Ephraim will become a desolation.

Question: On whom will Yahweh pour his wrath?
Response: Yahweh will pour his wrath on the leaders of Judah.

Question: When did Ephraim go to Assyria?
Response: When Ephraim saw his sickness, then he went to Assyria.

Question: For how long will Yahweh go and return to his place?
Response: Yahweh will go and return to his place until Ephraim and Judah acknowledge their guilt and seek his face; until they earnestly seek him in their distress.

Question: Although Yahweh tore his people to pieces, what will he then do?
Response: Although he tore them to pieces, he will heal them.

Question: When will Yahweh raise up his people?
Response: He will raise them up on the third day.

Question: What was the faithfulness of Ephraim and Judah like?
Response: Their faithfulness was like a morning cloud, like the dew that goes away early.

Question: What did God desire?
Response: God desired faithfulness and not sacrifice, and the knowledge of him more than burnt offerings.

Question: In what way did the priests band together to commit murder on the way to Shechem?
Response: As gangs of robbers wait for someone, so the priests banded together to commit murder on the way to Shechem.

Question: What was appointed to Judah when Yahweh will restore the fortunes of his people?
Response: A harvest was appointed, when Yahweh will restore the fortunes of his people.

Question: What did Yahweh remember?
Response: Yahweh remembered all their evil deeds.

Question: How did the people make the officials glad?
Response: By their lies they made the officials glad.

Question: What were the people’s hearts like?
Response: Their hearts were like an oven.

Question: What did Ephraim not know?
Response: Ephraim did not know that foreigners devoured his strength or that gray hairs were sprinkled on him.

Question: How was Ephraim like a dove?
Response: Ephraim was like a dove, gullible and without sense.

Question: Why did Yahweh not rescue the people?
Response: Yahweh did not rescue the people because they spoke lies against him.

Question: What did the people do to obtain grain and new wine?
Response: They cut themselves to obtain grain and new wine.

Question: Why will the officials fall?
Response: The officials will fall because of the wickedness of their tongues.

Question: Why was an eagle coming over the house of Yahweh?
Response: An eagle was coming over the house of Yahweh because the people broke his covenant and rebelled against his Law.

Question: With what did the people make idols for themselves?
Response: With their silver and gold they made idols for themselves.

Question: What happened when people sowed the wind?
Response: People sowed the wind and reaped the whirlwind.

Question: What will Yahweh do even though the people hired lovers among the nations?
Response: Even though they hired lovers among the nations, Yahweh will gather them together.

Question: If Yahweh wrote down his Law for them ten thousand times, how would they view it?
Response: If Yahweh wrote down his Law for them ten thousand times, they would view it as something strange to them.

Question: How will Yahweh punish the people’s sins?
Response: He will punish their sins by returning them to Egypt.

Question: What did Israel love to pay?
Response: Israel loved to pay the wages a prostitute required on all the threshing floors.

Question: What will Ephraim’s sacrifices be to them like?
Response: Their sacrifices will be to them like mourners’ food: all who eat it will be defiled.

Question: If the people escaped from destruction, what will happen?
Response: If the people escaped from destruction, Egypt will gather them, and Memphis will bury them.

Question: What days were coming?
Response: The days for punishment were coming. The days for retribution were coming.

Question: What was on the prophet’s paths?
Response: A bird’s snare was on his paths.

Question: After the fathers went to Baal Peor, what did they become like?
Response: After the fathers went to Baal Peor, they became as detestable as the idol they loved.

Question: What will happen to Ephraim’s glory?
Response: Ephraim’s glory will fly away like a bird.

Question: What will Yahweh give Ephraim?
Response: Yahweh will give them a miscarrying womb and breasts that give no milk.

Question: Why will Yahweh drive Ephraim out of his house?
Response: Because of their sinful deeds, Yahweh will drive them out of his house.

Question: What would happen even if Ephraim had children?
Response: Even if they had children, Yahweh would put their beloved children to death.

Question: What happened as Israel’s fruit increased?
Response: As his fruit increased, the more altars he built.

Question: How did Israel make covenants?
Response: Israel made covenants by swearing falsely.

Question: Why will the inhabitants of Samaria be carried to Assyria?
Response: They will be carried to Assyria for a present for the great king.

Question: What will grow over the altar?
Response: Thorns and thistles will grow over the altar.

Question: Why will Yahweh put a yoke on Ephraim’s fair neck?
Response: Because Ephraim was a trained heifer that loves to thresh the grain, Yahweh will put a yoke on her fair neck.

Question: For how long should the people seek Yahweh?
Response: They should seek Yahweh until he comes and rains righteousness on them.

Question: What happened to mothers with their children when Shalman destroyed Beth Arbel?
Response: When Shalman destroyed Beth Arbel, mothers were dashed to pieces with their children.

Question: From where did Yahweh call Israel when Israel was a young man?
Response: When Israel was a young man, Yahweh called him out of Egypt.

Question: Who lifted Ephraim up by their arms?
Response: Yahweh lifted them up by their arms.

Question: What were Yahweh’s people determined to do?
Response: Yahweh’s people were determined to turn away from him.

Question: Why will Yahweh not again destroy Ephraim?
Response: Yahweh will not again destroy Ephraim, for he is God and not a man.

Question: How will the people come?
Response: The people will come trembling from the west like a bird from Egypt, like a dove from the land of Assyria.

Question: Who was still faithful to God?
Response: Judah was still faithful to God.

Question: What did Yahweh have against Judah?
Response: Yahweh had a lawsuit against Judah.

Question: What did Jacob do in the womb?
Response: In the womb, Jacob grasped his brother by the heel.

Question: For what should the people wait?
Response: They should wait continually for their God.

Question: What will no one find in Ephraim’s work?
Response: In all Ephraim’s work no one will find any sin in him, anything that would be sin.

Question: How long had Yahweh been with the people?
Response: Yahweh had been with them from the land of Egypt.

Question: What will Gilgal’s altars be like?
Response: Gilgal’s altars will be like heaps of stone in the furrows of the fields.

Question: What did Yahweh do by a prophet?
Response: Yahweh brought Israel out of Egypt by a prophet, and by a prophet he took care of them.

Question: Why did Ephraim become guilty?
Response: He became guilty because of Baal worship.

Question: What happens to chaff?
Response: Chaff is driven by the wind away from a threshing floor.

Question: What happened when Yahweh’s people were filled?
Response: When Yahweh’s people were filled, their heart became lifted up and they forgot him.

Question: Like what will Yahweh attack his people?
Response: He will attack them like a bear that is robbed of her cubs.

Question: Why was Israel’s destruction coming?
Response: Israel’s destruction was coming because they were against Yahweh, their helper.

Question: When it was time for Ephraim to be born, what happened?
Response: When it was time for Ephraim to be born, he did not come out of the womb.

Question: What did Yahweh want brought to him?
Response: Yahweh wanted death’s plagues and Sheol’s destruction brought to him.

Question: What will Ephraim’s enemy plunder?
Response: His enemy will plunder the storehouse of every precious object.

Question: What will happen to Samaria’s young children?
Response: Their young children will be dashed to pieces.

Question: Why did Israel fall?
Response: Israel fell because of their sin.

Question: Why will Israel not say to the work of their hands, “You are our gods”?
Response: Israel will not say to the work of their hands, “You are our gods,” for in Yahweh the fatherless person finds compassion.

Question: When will Yahweh heal Israel?
Response: Yahweh will heal them when they turn back to him.

Question: What will the people’s fame be like?
Response: Their fame will be like the wine of Lebanon.

Question: Who will stumble in the ways of Yahweh?
Response: The rebellious will stumble in them.
